page: Amazon Web Services r8g.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps with no integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data highlights the instance's strong memory subsystem. It achieves top-tier performance in large-block memory bandwidth tests, both for read and write operations, and scores in the top tier for Passmark memory and database operations. CPU performance is balanced, with Geekbench multi-core scores reaching the top tier, while single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench remains in the average range. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as Redis SET operations and gzip compression, demonstrate strong performance, though large-model LLM inference text generation is relatively weak.

Given its high memory density and dedicated Graviton cores, the r8g.8xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The absence of hyperthreading ensures predictable per-core performance. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, including large-scale database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r8g.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by the arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs. Performance benchmarks show top-tier capabilities in memory bandwidth and database operations, alongside strong multi-core processing and Redis throughput, while single-core and LLM inference metrics remain average to weak. This hardware profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads by providing high memory density without the overhead of hyperthreading. The r8g.8xlarge is well-suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and high-throughput web serving.

tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring thirty-two dedicated cores and high-bandwidth memory for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated arm64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 at 2.8 GHz.
256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core.
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmark performance.
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included.
Optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and web serving.
